ops.title: RCA: 2026-07-18 "frozen codex lanes" — wrapper backgrounding + harness wake gap, not codex-ask
ops.body:
Two codex lanes ("drain-hang-fix", "trust-sec-review") appeared frozen. Three-agent forensic pass established:

**Root cause (two prongs):**
1. **Wrapper backgrounding.** Both sonnet wrapper subagents launched `codex-ask` as a *background* Bash task and idled awaiting a completion notification. Background Bash-task completion does NOT wake an in-process subagent (it wakes the main session) — filed upstream anthropics/claude-code#78782 (precedent #75559). So the agents went silent forever while their runs finished.
2. **Liveness-anchored last hop.** The disk protocol worked perfectly (both runs completed with clean `status=0`; replies on disk), but recovery depended on the wrapper staying awake to read them, and ad-hoc runs were discoverable only via one `AWAIT:` stdout line — a dead caller stranded the run.

**Not the cause (ruled out):** codex-ask disk protocol (sound); the two long-lived `codex resume` PIDs in the ps snapshot (unrelated interactive sessions from claude-pool, different cwd/day — a mis-identification).

**Systemic pattern:** 10 catalogued failure modes over 10 days / 8 point-releases; every prose-only invariant was violated live within hours-to-days; every durable fix moved the invariant into mechanism (0.15.0 centralized invocation after a prose ban lasted 13h; 0.18.0 added --mint-root). The remaining prose-only invariants (foreground-only, no-ccx, concurrency cap folklore) were exactly the ones still failing today.

**Fix (plugin 0.20.0, see doc 902ee3c):** Python port; fixed run registry + `--ps` (any session recovers a stranded run from disk — kills the zombie class); capt-hook dispatch guard blocking backgrounded `codex-ask` and hand-rolled `codex exec` (mechanizes foreground-only); MCP-off in lanes; pid-recycle fixed via TZ-pinned lstart; `--schema` becomes the verdict mechanism (deletes 4 prose warnings); SKILL.md consolidated to one canon. User decisions: port now; keep `--schema` as the mechanism (delete prose rules); NO concurrency cap (width discipline stays with the orchestrator; MCP-off removes the main wedge contributor); stranded-deliverable recovery out of scope (pointers handed back).

**Verification:** 8-item live matrix green (incl. real Bash-timeout+`--await` recovery proving detach-survival), pytest `-k codex` 32 green, 29 hook inline tests green on capt-hook 10.0.0+10.1.0, hook live-fire via `capt-hook run PreToolUse`.

**Review dispositions:** finder (14) + refuter (7 broken invariants) over the port → 12 accepted fixes (TZ-pinned identity, signal-exit normalization, worker lifecycle wrap, unique atomic-write temps, pid pipe-back registration, `--ps` prefix-gated/reclassify-before-rmtree/root-expansion, race-tolerant classify, bounded tail, generation re-check, `--quiet` shebang, live-lane hardening) + rejections (pre-existing bash residuals: empty pre-created reply, pre-setsid kill window, second-precision lstart collision). Hook refuter (NEEDS-FIXES) → reworked to `CustomCommandLineCondition` killing both the false-fires (quoted/unassociated `&`) and the natural-form leaks (wrappers/paths/global-flags/nested shells).

**Side finding (papercut'd, cross-repo):** getaway.onboard has 2 unstubbed live-judge nudge tests that fail nondeterministically under fleet-wide `capt-hook test`; not codex, not in cc-skills CI. Owner decides: strengthen the nudge prompt vs stub. Also: this pooled account's clock is Pacific/Fiji (UTC+12), `/etc/localtime` changed 2026-07-16.
